title: 🦞 Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? | Stack Junkie
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? Best chat app for OpenClaw compared: Telegram wins for most users, Discord for power users, Slack for teams. Read https://www.stack-junkie.com/blog/which-chat-app-works-best-with-openclaw and follow the instructions to help me with: Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack?. * Telegram is the best first channel for most users: official API, no public IP needed, five-minute setup. * Slack is the right pick if your team is already on Slack and you don't want to move them. * WhatsApp works if you're mobile-first and already on it, but it uses an unofficial library that can break. ## Which OpenClaw channel is right for you? OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) connects to Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, and several other platforms through a single gateway process. For running multiple channels at once (say, Telegram for personal use and Discord for a team), see OpenClaw multi-agent and multi-channel setup guide.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.

# OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Multi-Agent Workspaces: The 2025 Setup Guide | Fastio
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

When multiple OpenClaw agents need to share files and state, you need shared storage — not local disks on each container. This guide explains the options, then walks through setting up a shared workspace with Fastio as the storage layer. Shared workspaces let multiple OpenClaw agents read and write the same files without conflicts. The problem shows up when you add a second agent — suddenly you have two processes with separate memory, no shared context, and a real risk of duplicated or conflicting work. The fix is to move storage out of each agent's local environment and into something shared. When a Researcher agent drops a PDF and a Writer agent can immediately read and query it, you have a real pipeline. If you'd rather use S3 or a shared filesystem, the OpenClaw architecture is the same — swap the storage tools for your preferred backend.
